si.t: Acknowledgement of the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) as the source
The PSGC is being distributed without warranty of any kind, either expressed or implied.
The responsibility for the interpretation and use of the PSGC lies with the user. In no event shall the Philippine Statistics Authority be liable for damages arising from its use.
P R O V I N C I A L S U M M A R Y

NOTE: All cities shown on this table are Highly Urbanized Cities (HUCs) except for Isabela City (Component City) in Region IX and Cotabato City (Independent Component City) in Region XII, whose provinces (Basilan and Maguindanao) are under the regional jurisdiction of ARMM per E.O. No. 36.
Source of data: Local Treasury Operations Division, Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF)
P 360M or more but less than P 450M

P 15M or more but less than P 25M
City of San Jose Del Monte
Population counts for the regions do not add up to the national total. Includes 2,098 Filipinos in Philippine Embassies, Consulates and Mission Abroad.
2020 Census of Population and Housing includes 2,098 Filipinos in Philippine Embassies, Consulates and Mission Abroad.
2. Population counts do not add up to the national total.
Bangsamoro Autonomous Region In Muslim Mindanao (BARMM)
Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM)
Province Income Class was updated as of May 2015
City Income Class was updated as of June 2018
Municipality Income Class was updated as of April 2016

r.t: = not reclassified under Department Order No. 23-08 due to incomplete income data

Emerson M. Aquino / Von Jeric F. Adona / Emmanuel V. Prades
(excluding the cities of CEBU, LAPU-LAPU, AND MANDAUE)

2. While geographically located within the island province of Basilan, the City of Isabela is administratively classified as part of Region IX (Zamboanga Peninsula) and has been assigned a special province code.
10/ including the population count of newly created Barangay New Canaan
11/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per Prov. Ord. No. 2009-6-044 (Barangays 9, 10, 11, 16, 18, 25, and 28)
12/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per Prov. Ord. No. 2009-6-044 (Barangays 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 12, 13, 15, 17, 23, and 27)
13/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per Prov. Ord. No. 2009-6-044 (Barangays 14, 19, 20, 21, 22, 24, and 26)
14/ including the population count of newly created barangay Boganga II
15/ including the populoation count of barangay Datu Dalidigan
16/ including the population count of Barangay Muzon East, Muzon South, and Muzon West
17/ including the population count of 10 barangays transferred to City of Taguig
18/ excluding the population count of 10 barangays transferred from City of Makati
19/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Aniban I, III, and V)
20/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Aniban II and IV)
21/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Kaingin and Digman)
22/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Ligas I and II)
23/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Mabolo I, II, and III)
24/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Maliksi II and III)
25/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Mambog II and V)
26/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Niog I, II, and III)
28/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays P.F. Espiritu V and VI)
29/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Tabing Dagat, Campo Santo, and Daang Bukid)
30/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Real I and II)
32/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Sineguelasan, Banalo, and Alima)
33/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Talaba I, III, and VII)
34/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Talaba IV, V, and VI)
35/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Zapote I and II)
36/ sum of the population of the merged barangays per City Ordinance No. 275-2023 dated 28 March 2023 (Barangays Zapote III and IV)
- All barangays in the National Capital Region be automatically classified as urban
(1) If a barangay has a population size of 5,000 or more, then a barangay is considered urban, or
(2) If a barangay has at least one establishment with a minimum of 100 employees, a barangay is considered urban, or

(3) If a barangay has 5 or more establishments with a minimum of 10 employees, and 5 or more facilities, then a barangay is considered urban. Note that if the facility is not present in the barangay, presence of facilities within the two kilometer radius from the barangay hall is considered.
- The following are the categories used for classifying the urban barangays:

1. The 'sub-municipalities' or Municipal Districts of the City of Manila were established under Section 6 of RA 409, were adopted in the PSGC, and were given a special municipality code. It should be noted, however, that they are not officially recognized as municipalities.
37/ including the population count of Barangay Angoyao
38/ including the population count of Barangay Sultan Corobong
39/ including the population count of Barangay Sultan Panoroganan
40/ excluding the population count of Barangay Guintolan
41/ including the population count of Barangay Guintolan that was transferred to Municipality of Imelda, Zamboanga Sibugay

The PSGC is a systematic classification and coding of geographic areas in the country based on the four well-established hierarchical levels of geographical-political subdivisions of the country, namely, region, province, city or municipality, and barangay.

The PSGC is updated based on official changes occurring in the administrative structure of the country through Republic Acts and local ordinances ratified through plebiscites conducted by the COMELEC.

r.t: 2. Based on PSA Board Resolution No. 2017-098 -
Adoption of the Operational Definition of Urban Areas in the Philippines
2. Based on PSA Board Resolution No. 2017-100 -
Approving and Adopting the Official Concepts and Definitions for Statistical Purposes of the Selected Sectors: Prices, Population, Housing, and Tourism
Based on Department of Finance (DOF) Department Order No. 23-08 -
Prescribing the New Income Brackets for the Re-classification of Provinces, Cities and Municipalities and Amending for the Purpose Department of Finance Order No. 20-05, dated July 29, 2005
